use_case_name: Store Authorization Data in the Authorization Cache

structured.name: Store Authorization Data in the Authorization Cache
structured.objective: To store all the latest received IdTokens in the Authorization Cache.
structured.description:
This use case covers how the Charging Station autonomously stores a record of previously
presented identifiers that have been successfully authorized by the CSMS in the Authorization
Cache. (Successfully meaning: a response received on a message containing an IdToken)
scenario:
1. The Charging Station receives a AuthorizeResponse, ReserveNowRequest or
TransactionEventResponse response message from the CSMS.

2. The Cache is updated by the Charging Station using all received IdTokenInfo from the response
message from the CSMS.
structured.prerequisites:
An Authorization Cache is implemented and and the value of the AuthCacheEnabled
Configuration Variable is set to 'true'.
structured.postconditions:
Successful postcondition:
The Charging Station stored the newly received IdTokenInfo data in the Authorization Cache.
Failure postcondition:
The Charging Station was not able to store the Authorization Cache.

latest received identifiers (regardless of their
status).

Cache values SHOULD be persistent across reboots
and power outages.

requirements.precondition:

stored in the Authorization Cache with
status other than Accepted, and the
Charging Station is online.
requirements.definition:
AuthorizeRequest SHALL be sent to the CSMS to
check the current state of the IdToken.

The Charging Station SHALL update the
Authorisation Cache entry.

The Charging Station SHALL update the
Authorisation Cache entry.

The Charging Station SHALL have a mechanism to
accept new cache entries even when it is full, by
deleting older entries.

When IdTokenInfoType does not
contain a value for
cacheExpiryDateTime
requirements.definition:
The time a token is considered to be present in the
cache is determined by the Configuration Variable
AuthCacheLifeTime. This variable indicates how
long it takes until a token expires in the
Authorization Cache since it is last used.

requirements.precondition:
The Charging Station supports Tariff &
Cost
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL NOT store the tariff
information in the Cache.

requirements.precondition:
NOT C10.FR.13 AND
when more than
AuthCacheLifeTime seconds have
passed since idTokenInfo was last
updated
requirements.definition:
The Authorization Cache entry SHALL be removed
from the cache or changed to Expired.

Whether the Authorization Cache is enabled or
disabled SHALL be controlled by the
AuthCacheEnabled Configuration Variable.

It is RECOMMENDED to store personal information
in the Authorization Cache securely

requirements.precondition:
When IdTokenInfoType contains a
value for cacheExpiryDateTime and
current time is greater than
idTokenInfo.cacheExpiryDateTime
requirements.definition:
The Authorization Cache entry SHALL be removed
from the cache or changed to Expired.

chunks.content:
# C10 - Store Authorization Data in the Authorization Cache

**Objective:** To store all the latest received IdTokens in the Authorization Cache.

**Description:** This use case covers how the Charging Station autonomously stores a record of previously
presented identifiers that have been successfully authorized by the CSMS in the Authorization
Cache. (Successfully meaning: a response received on a message containing an IdToken)

**Actors:** Charging Station, CSMS

**Scenario:**
1. The Charging Station receives a AuthorizeResponse, ReserveNowRequest or
TransactionEventResponse response message from the CSMS.
2. The Cache is updated by the Charging Station using all received IdTokenInfo from the response
message from the CSMS.

**Prerequisites:** An Authorization Cache is implemented and and the value of the AuthCacheEnabled
Configuration Variable is set to 'true'.

**Postconditions:** Successful postcondition:
The Charging Station stored the newly received IdTokenInfo data in the Authorization Cache.
Failure postcondition:
The Charging Station was not able to store the Authorization Cache.

metadata.heading: C10 - Store Authorization Data in the Authorization Cache
metadata.use_case_name: Store Authorization Data in the Authorization Cache

chunks.content:
# C10 - Requirements

**C10.FR.01**
The Authorization Cache SHALL contain all the
latest received identifiers (regardless of their
status).

**C10.FR.02**
Cache values SHOULD be persistent across reboots
and power outages.

**C10.FR.03**
Precondition: When an IdToken is presented that is
stored in the Authorization Cache with
status other than Accepted, and the
Charging Station is online.
AuthorizeRequest SHALL be sent to the CSMS to
check the current state of the IdToken.

**C10.FR.04**
Precondition: Upon receipt of AuthorizeResponse.
The Charging Station SHALL update the
Authorisation Cache entry.

**C10.FR.05**
Precondition: Upon receipt of
TransactionEventResponse.
The Charging Station SHALL update the
Authorisation Cache entry.

**C10.FR.07**
The Charging Station SHALL have a mechanism to
accept new cache entries even when it is full, by
deleting older entries.

**C10.FR.08**
Precondition: When IdTokenInfoType does not
contain a value for
cacheExpiryDateTime
The time a token is considered to be present in the
cache is determined by the Configuration Variable
AuthCacheLifeTime. This variable indicates how
long it takes until a token expires in the
Authorization Cache since it is last used.

**C10.FR.09**
Precondition: The Charging Station supports Tariff &
Cost
The Charging Station SHALL NOT store the tariff
information in the Cache.

**C10.FR.10**
Precondition: NOT C10.FR.13 AND
when more than
AuthCacheLifeTime seconds have
passed since idTokenInfo was last
updated
The Authorization Cache entry SHALL be removed
from the cache or changed to Expired.

**C10.FR.11**
Whether the Authorization Cache is enabled or
disabled SHALL be controlled by the
AuthCacheEnabled Configuration Variable.

**C10.FR.12**
It is RECOMMENDED to store personal information
in the Authorization Cache securely

**C10.FR.13**
Precondition: When IdTokenInfoType contains a
value for cacheExpiryDateTime and
current time is greater than
idTokenInfo.cacheExpiryDateTime
The Authorization Cache entry SHALL be removed
from the cache or changed to Expired.

metadata.heading: C10 - Requirements
